mission.title: k3s: cannot run k3s server
mission.description: cannot run k3s server. Users encounter: "code = Unavailable desc = all SubConns are in TransientFailure, latest connection error: connection".

steps.title: Identify k3s troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your k3s deployment:
```bash
k3s --version
systemctl status k3s
journalctl -u k3s --no-pager --lines=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `code = Unavailable desc = all SubConns are in TransientFailure, latest connection error: connection error: desc = "trans`

steps.title: Review k3s configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:
```bash
k3s --version
kubectl get nodes -o wide
kubectl get pods -A
```
I just run k3s server. But got the error. "Waiting for containerd startup: rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = all SubConns are in TransientFailure, latest connection error: connection error: desc = "transport: Error while dialing dial unix

steps.title: Apply the fix for cannot run k3s server
steps.description:
I had the same issue on Ubuntu 16.04.5 LTS with Ansible on Vagrant/VirtualBox. Strangely sometimes it worked, other times with connect: connection refused error on containerd.sock. 

But I found out that restarting the k3s service using `systemctl restart k3s` seems a workaround. Altough the error
```yaml
curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -
    # Check for Ready node, takes maybe 30 seconds
    k3s kubectl get node
```

steps.title: Confirm cannot run k3s server is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
journalctl -u k3s --no-pager --lines=50 --since='5 minutes ago'
kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `code = Unavailable desc = all SubConns are in TransientFailure, latest connection error: connection error: desc = "trans` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
I had the same issue on Ubuntu 16.04.5 LTS with Ansible on Vagrant/VirtualBox. Strangely sometimes it worked, other times with connect: connection refused error on containerd.sock. 

But I found out that restarting the k3s service using `systemctl restart k3s` seems a workaround. Altough the error still contains in the logs after restart, it seems only temprorary.

prerequisites.description: A running k3s cluster or a system where k3s can be installed.
